name: Pico del Nevero Loop from Navafría
name_original: Puerto de Navafría, Pico del Nevero y Cerro Pelado — circular desde Navafría por Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
description: This difficult 26.5-mile mountain bike route in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park climbs to Pico del Nevero, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Navafría offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, providing a varied landscape for riders. The region features the extensive Pinar de Navafría, a Scots pine forest, which provides shaded trails and scenic environments. Riders encounter mountain passes like Puerto de Navafría and routes along the River Cega, contributing to the area's natural features. This combination of forest, mountain, and river environments creates a network of trails suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Navafría The most popular mountain bike trail is La Horizontal Trail, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the picturesque Lozoya Valley within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pico del Nevero Loop from Navafría, a difficult 26.5 miles (42.7 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local mountain bikers also love the Navafría Pass – Puerto de Linera loop from Gallegos, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail leading through mountain passes and forests, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Navafría is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, the Pinar de Navafría forest, and significant mountain passes. The extensive network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Navafría are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navafría's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Navafría?
A: Navafría offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 70 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Navafría?
A: The terrain in Navafría is diverse, primarily featuring the extensive Scots pine forest of Pinar de Navafría, which provides shaded trails. You'll also encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt; and routes along the River Cega, offering a mix of forest paths, mountain climbs, and riverside trails. The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park setting ensures varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Navafría?
A: Yes, Navafría has 13 easy mountain bike routes, perfect for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails often wind through the scenic Pinar de Navafría, offering a pleasant and accessible experience.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Navafría?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Navafría offers 27 difficult mountain bike routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079486696/pico-del-nevero-loop-from-navafria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero Loop from Navafría&lt;/a&gt; provide significant climbs and panoramic views, testing your endurance and skill.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Navafría?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Navafría are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Linera loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountain passes and forests in a complete loop.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Navafría?
A: While mountain biking in Navafría, you can encounter several natural attractions. The region is home to the impressive Cascada del Chorro (waterfall) within the El Chorro Recreational Area. You might also pass by significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt; and mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Navafría?
A: The mountain bike trails in Navafría are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 120 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Pinar de Navafría, and the challenging climbs that lead to spectacular panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Is it possible to reach Navafría by public transport for mountain biking?
A: Navafría is a rural mountain village. While specific public transport options for mountain bikers carrying bikes might be limited, it's advisable to check local bus schedules from nearby larger towns like Segovia or Madrid. However, many visitors prefer to drive to the area for greater flexibility with their equipment.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park around Navafría?
A: Mountain biking within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park generally follows specific regulations to protect the natural environment. While many established trails are open, it's always recommended to check the official National Park guidelines for any specific permit requirements or restricted areas, especially for off-trail riding.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navafría?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Navafría is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the shaded pine forests, but it's wise to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter riding is possible but may involve snow and colder temperatures, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Puerto de Navafría?
A: Yes, the Puerto de Navafría is a significant feature in the region and several routes incorporate it. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Navalcollado Viewpoint loop from Puerto de Navafría&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that starts and finishes at the pass, offering extensive climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay in Navafría village after a ride?
A: Navafría is a charming rural village with traditional stone houses. You can find local cafes, pubs, and restaurants to refuel after your mountain bike adventure. There are also rural accommodations available, offering a cozy stay in the heart of the Sierra de Guadarrama.
tips: An ideal place to stop and regain strength while enjoying wonderful views from the Navalcollado viewpoint.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Puerto de Navafría, Pico del Nevero y Cerro Pelado mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts and ends near Navafría. You'll likely find parking options within or on the outskirts of Navafría village, which serves as the primary access point for this trail.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging route rated as a difficult mountain bike tour. It requires good physical condition and technical skill due to significant ascents, demanding descents, and varied terrain, including some overgrown singletrack and rocky sections. It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter forest tracks, some 'asilvestrado' (wild or overgrown) singletrack sections, and even some 'freeride' segments. There are significant climbs, like the 13 km ascent to Pico Nevero, and substantial descents, which can include rocky parts. One section even requires dismounting to pass a narrow gate.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to spectacular views throughout the ride. Key highlights include the Navalcollado Viewpoint and the summit of Nevero Peak, which offers panoramic vistas of the Lozoya Valley, Peñalara, Cuerda Larga, and the Segovian plain. You'll also pass through magnificent pine forests and near a Stone Bridge on Forest Road.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this route is located within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check the latest national park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama for activities like mountain biking. However, certain activities or specific areas might require permits. For this particular route, standard mountain biking typically does not require a special permit, but it's always wise to check the official park website for any updated regulations before your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically during late spring, summer, and early autumn. Winter can bring snow and ice, making many sections impassable or extremely challenging. Be aware that high-altitude sections, especially around Pico del Nevero, can experience rapidly changing weather conditions, even in warmer months.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Ruta del Sureste, the Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, and various Pista esquí de fondo Navafría trails, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or sections I should be aware of on this trail?
faqs.answer: Beyond the overall difficulty, be prepared for the long, sustained climb to Pico del Nevero. Some sections are described as very demanding, even for hiking, and parts of the trail can be overgrown ('asilvestrado'). The descents can be rocky and require technical skill. An e-bike might make the ascents more manageable, but the technical demands remain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Navafría?
faqs.answer: Yes, Navafría is a small village and you should be able to find local cafes or restaurants there to refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check their opening hours, especially if you're riding early or late in the day.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from Pico del Nevero?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Nevero Peak, you can expect truly breathtaking panoramic views. You'll see the expansive Lozoya Valley, the iconic silhouette of Peñalara (the highest peak in Guadarrama), the Cuerda Larga mountain range, and the vast Segovian plain stretching out to the north.
